description: A designed 'First 100 Days' accomplishments quote-card from second-Trump-administration USCIS, on a white background. Top left: bold navy heading 'First 100 Days' with red italic subhead 'Accomplishments'; top right: the full-color U.S. Department of Homeland Security seal beside the agency wordmark 'U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services'. The body is a large italicized navy serif quotation: '“In the first 100 days, USCIS put a stop to disastrous Biden-era ‘humanitarian’ policies that invited fraud and allowed criminal aliens to legally live and work in our communities; facilitated arrests of criminals attempting to gain immigration benefits; and for the first time in decades is ensuring every alien in the U.S. is registered as required by law,” said USCIS Spokesman Matthew Tragesser. “Aliens, immigration attorneys and non-government organizations take note: the days of exploiting our immigration system are over. Aliens who want to live and work in America need to do it legally or get out.”' At the bottom are decorative red and navy underline rule marks. The visual style is clean and official (agency seal, two-color typographic scheme). Messaging technique: a triumphalist spokesperson quote that disparages prior 'Biden-era humanitarian policies', conflates immigrants with 'criminal aliens' and fraud, and issues a blunt warning ('do it legally or get out') aimed explicitly at aliens, immigration attorneys, and NGOs. Notably, this is the hardline ideological counterpart to the 2021 Tracy Renaud welcoming quote-card elsewhere in this dataset. Target audience: the public and immigration stakeholders.
notable_text: First 100 Days Accomplishments; '...the days of exploiting our immigration system are over. Aliens who want to live and work in America need to do it legally or get out.' — USCIS Spokesman Matthew Tragesser
